paper_title: Algal response to multi-driver global change scenarios

methods.description: Procedure for culturing the diatom T. weissflogii under different temperature, pCO2, and nutrient scenarios based on IPCC predictions, including stock culture maintenance, experimental setup, and various sampling procedures.

steps.description: Maintain a single strain stock culture of T. weissflogii (SAG, strain number 122.79) in semi-continuous dilute-batch culture, diluted by 95% every 4-5 days with f/2 medium prepared with artificial seawater.
steps.searchable_summary: maintain algal stock culture using semi-continuous dilute-batch method

steps.description: Maintain the stock culture under a 14:10 day:night cycle at 14˚C and irradiance of 95μmol photons m -2 s -1 (GHL Mitras Lightbar 2 Daylight, 6500K, dimmed to 80%).
steps.searchable_summary: control light and temperature conditions for algal stock culture
outputs: Stock culture under controlled environmental conditions

steps.description: Gently homogenize all stock culture bottles manually several times a day to prevent sedimentation.
steps.searchable_summary: homogenize algal stock cultures

steps.description: Prepare experimental culture medium with artificial seawater based on f/2 medium, setting N concentration to 880 μmol L -1 and P concentrations to 54 μmol L -1 (N:P 16) and 35 μmol L -1 (N:P 25) for the two nutrient scenarios.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare experimental culture medium with specific nutrient concentrations
outputs: Experimental culture medium for N:P 16 and N:P 25 treatments

steps.description: Conduct the experiment in temperature-controlled rooms set to 14.0, 15.5, and 17.0˚C to maintain constant temperatures for the three IPCC scenarios (ambient, RCP6.0, RCP8.5).
steps.searchable_summary: set up temperature control for experimental treatments

steps.description: Aerate the culture medium with a mixture of air stripped of CO2 by soda lime and pure CO2 for 24 h before inoculation to reach the desired pCO2 levels for the three IPCC scenarios (ambient ~450ppm, RCP6.0 ~800ppm, RCP8.5 ~1000ppm).
steps.searchable_summary: control pCO2 levels in culture medium via aeration
outputs: Culture medium with target pCO2 levels

steps.description: Initiate the experiment by inoculating 1L glass bottles with the stock culture at a starting cell density of 1000 cells ml -1 in six biological replicates for each of the six treatments (3 temperature/pCO2 x 2 N:P), yielding a total of 36 bottles.
steps.searchable_summary: inoculate experimental batch cultures

steps.description: Grow T. weissflogii in batch cultures for 10 days under the same light conditions as the stock culture (14:10 day:night, 95μmol photons m -2 s -1).
steps.searchable_summary: grow algal batch cultures under controlled light conditions

steps.description: Take samples for total alkalinity at early (Day 5) and late (Day 10) stationary phase, using two of the six replicates for both analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: sample algal cultures for total alkalinity measurement

steps.description: Filter 125ml from each alkalinity sample with a 0.45μm nylon filter (Sartorius).
steps.searchable_summary: filter alkalinity samples

steps.description: Store filtered alkalinity samples airtight in borosilicate bottles at 4˚C.
steps.searchable_summary: store filtered alkalinity samples

steps.description: Calculate the initial pCO2 in treatments and pCO2 at early (Day 5) and late (Day 10) stationary phase using the software CO2calc v4.0.9 based on the measured alkalinity and the corresponding pH.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate pCO2 from alkalinity and pH using CO2calc

steps.description: Measure macronutrients at the same time points as alkalinity samples (early and late stationary phase).
steps.searchable_summary: measure macronutrient concentrations in culture samples

steps.description: Sample two milliliters of each biological replicate daily one hour after artificial sunrise.
steps.searchable_summary: collect daily culture samples for flow cytometry

steps.description: Analyze daily samples directly via flow cytometry to evaluate how different traits are linked during different growth phases.
steps.searchable_summary: perform flow cytometry analysis on daily samples

steps.description: Fix daily samples immediately after flow cytometry analysis with acidic Lugol's iodine solution at a final concentration of 2%.
steps.searchable_summary: fix daily culture samples with Lugol's iodine
inputs: Daily culture samples after flow cytometry

steps.description: Store fixed samples in the dark for subsequent flow cytometric analyses of cellular lipid and protein content.
steps.searchable_summary: store fixed samples for later analysis

methods.description: Statistical and data processing procedures applied to the collected flow cytometry and other experimental data to analyze treatment effects, correlations, and variability.

steps.description: Use the software R for all statistical analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: initialize R environment for data analysis

steps.description: Test for differences between the treatments using analysis of variance (two-way ANOVA), with IPCC scenario (ambient, 6.0, and 8.5) and nutrient scenario as independent factors.
steps.searchable_summary: perform two-way ANOVA on experimental data

steps.description: Perform Tukey's (HSD) post-hoc comparisons following ANOVA to identify specific group differences.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Tukey HSD post-hoc test

steps.description: Perform Shapiro test for normality assumption check for statistical comparisons.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Shapiro-Wilk normality test

steps.description: Perform Bartlett test for variance homogeneity assumption check for statistical comparisons.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Bartlett test for homogeneity of variances

steps.description: Transform data (square root) if criteria of a normal distribution or variance homogeneity were not met.
steps.searchable_summary: apply square root transformation to data
inputs: Data failing normality or variance homogeneity tests

steps.description: If a transformation of data did not allow the use of ANOVA, perform the non-parametric Kruskal-Wallis test.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Kruskal-Wallis non-parametric test
inputs: Data not suitable for ANOVA after transformation

steps.description: If Kruskal-Wallis test was performed, use a multi comparison test from the package pgirmess for post-hoc comparison.
steps.searchable_summary: perform non-parametric post-hoc test using pgirmess package

steps.description: Use a principal component analysis (PCA) to test for the correlations between cellular parameters of T. weissflogii by using the results from the flow cytometric measurements.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Principal Component Analysis (PCA) on flow cytometry data

steps.description: Group the observations in equal numbers of observations starting at the low growth rates to investigate the effect of growth rate on the strain variance.
steps.searchable_summary: group data by growth rate into equal sized bins

steps.description: Set all negative growth rates to zero for the grouping analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: set negative growth rates to zero
outputs: Growth rate data with non-negative values

steps.description: Extract the values of the variance of each parameter within each growth rate group.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate variance of parameters within growth rate groups
outputs: Variance values per group per parameter

steps.description: Analyze the linear correlation between growth rate and the cell size variability.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze linear correlation between growth rate and cell size variability

steps.description: Test for differences between the slopes of the linear correlation between growth rate and cell size variability for the six treatments using libraries lsmeans, psych, and data.table.
steps.searchable_summary: test for differences in slopes of linear models
inputs: Linear correlation models for each treatment

steps.description: Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient R 2 of the linear correlation between growth rate and cell size variability for each of the six treatments using libraries lsmeans, psych, and data.table.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Pearson correlation coefficient (R^2)

steps.description: Fix the significance level of all comparisons at 95% (p = 0.05).
steps.searchable_summary: set significance level for statistical tests
